title: ornithopus uncinatus
intro: Ornithopus uncinatus, commonly known as subclover, is a perennial legume native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Ornithopus uncinatus include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help prevent infections. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its dried leaves and flowers can be used in salves and creams for topical applications.
